refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR bundles three distinct obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate functional concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies a specific DP) and focuses on the core problem: making spatial features available in a structured, queryable form. Splitting into atomic sub-FRs allows independent verification and design of each capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Confirm that a worksite model exists in a defined schema/format, contains spatial coordinates and feature classifications, and is accessible to downstream planning and execution functions per acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface properties across the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and locate static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and operational areas.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Integrate captured spatial features into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution functions.
refined_fr.note: The candidate FR is not atomic; it decomposes into four atomic sub-FRs, each addressing a single functional obligation. Each sub-FR can now be independently mapped to design parameters and implementation modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data via multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured spatial graph with feature attributes (geometry, classification, safety zone) queryable by planning/execution functions.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign feature capture responsibility to zone-specific acquisition modules; merge zone models via spatial registration and conflict resolution; expose unified model through a feature query interface.

DP3: Implement a real-time streaming pipeline that ingests raw sensor observations, applies incremental feature extraction and classification, and maintains a versioned spatial database; provide read-only snapshots and change notifications to downstream consumers.

DP4: Establish a declarative feature schema (geometry primitives, safety attributes, operational constraints) and enforce capture/representation compliance through schema validation; decouple acquisition from representation by normalizing all inputs to schema-compliant records.

DP5: Acquire raw spatial data; apply rule-based or learned feature detection to identify and classify worksite elements; store results in a hierarchical spatial index (octree, quadtree) with lazy evaluation of derived features on query.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP4 + DP3 (hybrid): Establish a declarative feature schema that defines geometry primitives, safety attributes, and operational constraints; enforce schema compliance through validation. Implement a real-time streaming acquisition and incremental feature extraction pipeline that normalizes all sensor inputs to schema-compliant records and maintains a versioned spatial database. Expose the model through a feature query interface with read-only snapshots and change notifications, decoupling acquisition from representation and enabling independent evolution of sensing modalities and downstream planning/execution consumers.

potential_sources: Schema evolution: Changes to feature schema may require updates to acquisition and query interfaces; mitigate via schema versioning and backward-compatible extensions.

Spatial registration: Multi-modal sensor fusion and zone merging (if DP2 is later composed) introduce coordinate frame coupling; mitigate via explicit registration module with published transformation APIs.
Real-time latency: Streaming pipeline may create tight coupling between acquisition rate and query freshness expectations; mitigate via explicit SLA definition and buffering strategy.
Feature classification logic: Learned or rule-based detection may drift; mitigate via decoupling detection from storage (detection is a DP detail, not a schema requirement).
coupling_check.notes: DP4 + DP3 achieves loose coupling by separating concerns: schema defines the contract (what features must be represented), streaming pipeline handles the mechanism (how data flows), and query interface isolates consumers from internal representation. Acquisition modality changes do not affect downstream planning/execution as long as schema compliance is maintained.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite features can be meaningfully classified and represented within a single unified coordinate frame (no fragmented or dynamic reference systems).
ASSUMPTION: Acceptance criteria 'agreed set of site features' implies a pre-defined feature taxonomy; if taxonomy is unknown or evolves frequently, schema versioning overhead increases.
RISK: Real-time streaming introduces operational complexity (buffering, backpressure, failure recovery); if latency tolerance is high, batch-based acquisition may be simpler.

RISK: Feature classification accuracy depends on sensor modality and environmental conditions (lighting, occlusion, weather); DP does not specify fallback or confidence thresholds; recommend explicit quality-of-service contract.

RISK: Spatial database consistency under concurrent updates (if multiple acquisition sources feed the pipeline) requires explicit concurrency control; DP assumes single-writer or eventual consistency model.

RISK: Safety-critical features (e.g., hazard zones) must be validated against operational policies; DP does not specify validation logic; recommend explicit policy enforcement module downstream of query interface.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP4 + DP3 to explicitly address: (1) Schema versioning and evolution strategy; (2) Spatial registration and coordinate frame management (if multi-modal or multi-zone); (3) Feature classification confidence/quality metrics and fallback behavior; (4) Concurrency control and consistency guarantees for spatial database; (5) Policy validation interface (safety plan and operational policies are applied by downstream planning/execution, not by the model itself, but the model must expose sufficient metadata for validation). Add a secondary DP for incremental model updates and change notification semantics to support real-time planning/execution feedback loops.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized declarative feature schema definition, real-time sensor acquisition pipeline, incremental feature extraction, schema validation, versioned spatial database, and feature query interface with change notifications. Owns the canonical representation of all sensor-derived geometry primitives, safety attributes, and operational constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests raw sensor streams (geometry, safety attributes, operational state) from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; normalized to schema-compliant records within NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits constraint violations and safety attribute changes detected during schema validation and feature extraction; consumed by Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Exposes read-only feature snapshots and change notifications to downstream planning/execution consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation); enables independent evolution of sensing and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports distributed query access to versioned spatial database and change notification subscriptions for remote planning/execution agents.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. NGHE Solution is the natural home for the declarative schema, validation logic, feature extraction pipeline, and spatial database. All core DP logic (schema definition, normalization, versioning, query interface) resides here. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry ingestion (in), SafetyAlert emission (out), Tasking exposure (out), and Network Link access (inout) cross boundaries—all well-defined and decoupling-oriented.

Poor fit. Vehicle is a consumer of features, not an owner of the canonical schema or database. Placing schema, validation, and spatial database here would couple Vehicle tightly to all sensing modalities and planning/execution logic. Feature extraction and versioning would diffuse across Vehicle and other subsystems.

Poor fit. Teleoperation is a control/interface subsystem, not a data representation owner. Placing the schema and spatial database here would create asymmetric coupling: Vehicle and Safety Services would depend on Teleoperation for feature access. Feature extraction and validation logic would diffuse across multiple subsystems.

Weak fit. Safety Services consumes safety attributes and constraint violations but does not own the canonical schema or spatial database. Placing the full DP here would couple Safety Services to all sensor acquisition and feature extraction logic, violating separation of concerns. Safety Services should react to SafetyAlert notifications, not manage the underlying feature model.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the single authoritative owner of the declarative feature schema, real-time acquisition pipeline, incremental feature extraction, schema validation, versioned spatial database, and feature query interface. This placement ensures: (1) cohesion—all schema definition, normalization, validation, and versioning logic is co-located; (2) decoupling—Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services consume features via well-defined read-only interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) without knowledge of internal acquisition or storage; (3) independent evolution—sensing modalities and downstream consumers can evolve independently by adhering to the schema contract.
containment_risks: NGHE Solution becomes a critical hub: all sensor data flows through it, and all downstream consumers depend on its availability and correctness. Mitigation: implement robust versioning, change notification subscriptions, and read-only snapshots to enable resilience and offline operation.

Schema evolution: changes to the declarative schema may require coordination with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Mitigation: use schema versioning and backward-compatible extensions; expose schema metadata via Tasking interface.

Real-time performance: incremental feature extraction and spatial database queries must meet latency requirements for planning/execution. Mitigation: profile and optimize the pipeline; consider caching and indexing strategies within NGHE Solution.

Distributed consistency: if Network Link enables remote access to the spatial database, eventual consistency and conflict resolution must be addressed. Mitigation: design the versioned database with clear semantics for concurrent updates and snapshot isolation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR ('Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model') is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: (1) 'A worksite model/map is generated and available to planning/execution functions for an agreed set of site features' maps to the Tasking interface (read-only snapshots and change notifications); (2) 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' maps to schema-compliant safety attributes and operational constraints validated and exposed via SafetyAlert. The IM boundary (NGHE Solution) owns the canonical representation, making traceability from FR through DP to IM clear and testable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Excellent. NGHE Solution is unambiguously allocated as the single authoritative owner of schema definition, validation, feature extraction, versioning, and query interface. External interfaces (Telemetry in, SafetyAlert out, Tasking out, Network Link inout) are well-defined and decoupling-oriented. No ambiguity about which subsystem owns which aspect of the FR.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP (hybrid DP4 + DP3) comprehensively addresses the FR. The declarative feature schema directly solves 'represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model'; real-time streaming acquisition and incremental feature extraction solve 'capture'; schema validation and versioned spatial database solve 'machine-readable and persistent'; feature query interface with read-only snapshots and change notifications solve 'available to planning/execution functions.' The DP explicitly decouples acquisition from representation and enables independent evolution of sensing modalities and downstream consumers, which is a sound logical solution to the FR. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry ingestion (in): Raw sensor normalization occurs at the NGHE Solution boundary. Risk: if sensor-specific parsing logic is scattered across Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems before reaching NGHE Solution, normalization becomes diffused. Mitigation: enforce that all raw telemetry is ingested as-is; parsing and normalization are owned exclusively by NGHE Solution.

SafetyAlert emission (out): Safety Services consumes constraint violations. Risk: if Safety Services implements its own schema interpretation or re-validates constraints, the canonical schema authority diffuses. Mitigation: SafetyAlert must be the sole source of truth for constraint violations; Safety Services reacts only, does not re-derive.

Tasking interface (out): Vehicle and Teleoperation consume feature snapshots and change notifications. Risk: if downstream consumers cache or transform features locally without versioning alignment, divergence occurs. Mitigation: enforce read-only semantics; provide explicit versioning metadata in snapshots; require consumers to subscribe to change notifications for consistency.

Network Link (inout): Distributed query access to versioned spatial database. Risk: remote agents may experience stale reads or concurrent update conflicts if consistency semantics are not explicit. Mitigation: document snapshot isolation guarantees; define conflict resolution for concurrent updates; consider eventual consistency with explicit version causality.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment score of 0.92 is justified. NGHE Solution is the natural and necessary home for the declarative schema, validation logic, feature extraction pipeline, and spatial database. All core DP logic resides here with minimal boundary crossing. The four external interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined, decoupling-oriented, and necessary for the system to function. No alternative allocation (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) would improve cohesion or reduce coupling. The 0.08 diffusion score reflects only the unavoidable interface crossings, which are intentional and well-scoped. Recommended: monitor real-time performance of the acquisition pipeline and spatial database queries to ensure latency requirements are met; implement robust versioning and change notification subscriptions to enable resilience and offline operation for downstream consumers.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document schema versioning strategy and backward-compatibility guarantees. Specify how schema evolution is communicated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services via the Tasking interface (e.g., schema metadata endpoint, deprecation timelines).

Define consistency semantics for the versioned spatial database, especially for Network Link distributed access. Specify snapshot isolation guarantees, conflict resolution for concurrent updates, and eventual consistency guarantees if applicable.

Establish performance acceptance criteria for the real-time acquisition pipeline and feature query interface (e.g., latency bounds for incremental feature extraction, query response times). Profile and optimize the pipeline to meet these criteria.

Clarify the scope of 'agreed set of site features' in the acceptance criterion. Specify which geometry primitives, safety attributes, and operational constraints are in scope for the initial release and how the schema accommodates future extensions.
Document the change notification subscription model (e.g., pub-sub semantics, filtering, delivery guarantees) to ensure downstream consumers can reliably track feature updates.

Define the boundary between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation for sensor calibration and quality assurance. Specify whether sensor validation (e.g., outlier detection, calibration checks) occurs in NGHE Solution or upstream in the sensor subsystems.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence and excellent allocation clarity. The FR is well-scoped, the DP is logically sound and comprehensive, and the IM boundary (NGHE Solution) is unambiguous with clear external interfaces. However, the safety criticality of this system (high) and the complexity of the real-time acquisition pipeline, schema validation, versioning, and distributed spatial database warrant decomposition into sub-FRs to ensure that critical concerns—particularly safety constraint enforcement, data consistency, and sensor quality assurance—are explicitly addressed at the next level. The recommended revisions identify several design decisions (schema versioning, consistency semantics, performance criteria, change notification model, sensor validation boundary) that are too important to defer; they should be captured as sub-level FRs to drive detailed design and verification. Decomposition will also enable independent ownership and testing of the acquisition pipeline, schema validation, spatial database, and interface management.
next_level_sub_frs: FR 1.1: Acquire and normalize raw sensor telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems in real-time, ensuring sensor-specific parsing and quality assurance occur exclusively within NGHE Solution boundary.

FR 1.2: Validate captured worksite features against the declarative schema and operational constraints, and emit SafetyAlert notifications for constraint violations with authoritative, non-redundant constraint interpretation.

FR 1.3: Maintain a versioned spatial database of worksite features with explicit snapshot isolation, change notification subscriptions, and conflict resolution semantics for distributed (Network Link) and local access.

FR 1.4: Provide read-only feature snapshots and change notifications to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems via the Tasking interface, with explicit versioning metadata and backward-compatibility guarantees for schema evolution.

FR 1.5: Define and communicate schema versioning strategy, backward-compatibility guarantees, and future extension mechanisms to all downstream consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) via schema metadata endpoints and deprecation timelines.

revise.why: The current DP (hybrid DP4 + DP3) is logically sound but lacks explicit design decisions on five critical concerns identified in recommended_revisions: (1) schema versioning and backward-compatibility strategy, (2) consistency semantics and conflict resolution for the versioned spatial database, (3) performance acceptance criteria and latency bounds for the acquisition pipeline and query interface, (4) scope definition for 'agreed set of site features' and schema extension mechanisms, and (5) change notification subscription model (pub-sub semantics, filtering, delivery guarantees). These should be elevated to explicit DP sub-components or design constraints to ensure they are addressed during detailed design and are verifiable. Recommend: augment the DP with explicit sub-DPs for schema versioning (e.g., semantic versioning with deprecation timelines), consistency model (e.g., snapshot isolation with eventual consistency and version causality), performance targets (e.g., <100ms latency for incremental feature extraction, <500ms for query responses), and change notification semantics (e.g., pub-sub with at-least-once delivery and filtering by feature type/region).

assumptions: NGHE Solution has exclusive ownership of schema definition, validation, feature extraction, versioning, and spatial database. No other subsystem re-interprets or re-validates the schema.
Telemetry ingestion occurs at the NGHE Solution boundary; all raw sensor normalization and parsing are owned by NGHE Solution, not by Vehicle or Teleoperation.
SafetyAlert is the sole source of truth for constraint violations. Safety Services reacts to alerts but does not re-derive or re-validate constraints.
Tasking interface enforces read-only semantics for feature snapshots. Downstream consumers do not cache or transform features without versioning alignment.
Network Link provides distributed query access with explicit consistency semantics (snapshot isolation, eventual consistency with version causality).
Schema evolution is communicated proactively to all downstream consumers via metadata endpoints and deprecation timelines.
Sensor quality assurance (outlier detection, calibration checks) occurs within NGHE Solution, not upstream in Vehicle or Teleoperation sensor subsystems.
Real-time acquisition pipeline and spatial database queries meet latency requirements (to be defined in sub-level FRs).
open_risks: Telemetry ingestion diffusion: If Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems implement sensor-specific parsing before sending to NGHE Solution, normalization authority diffuses. Mitigation: enforce strict interface contract requiring raw telemetry only.

SafetyAlert re-interpretation: If Safety Services implements its own schema interpretation or re-validates constraints, canonical authority diffuses. Mitigation: SafetyAlert must be sole source of truth; Safety Services reacts only.

Tasking interface caching divergence: If downstream consumers cache or transform features locally without versioning alignment, feature consistency diverges. Mitigation: enforce read-only semantics, provide explicit versioning metadata, require change notification subscriptions.

Network Link consistency: Distributed query access may experience stale reads or concurrent update conflicts if consistency semantics are not explicit. Mitigation: document snapshot isolation guarantees, define conflict resolution, specify eventual consistency with version causality.

Schema versioning complexity: If schema evolution is not communicated clearly or backward-compatibility is not guaranteed, downstream consumers may break. Mitigation: establish versioning strategy, deprecation timelines, and metadata endpoints.

Sensor quality assurance boundary: If sensor validation (outlier detection, calibration) is split between Vehicle/Teleoperation and NGHE Solution, quality assurance becomes diffused. Mitigation: clarify boundary and enforce exclusive ownership in NGHE Solution.

Performance under load: Real-time acquisition pipeline and spatial database queries may not meet latency requirements under high sensor data rates or large feature sets. Mitigation: establish performance acceptance criteria, profile and optimize pipeline.
evidence_needed: Detailed schema versioning strategy document with semantic versioning scheme, backward-compatibility guarantees, and deprecation timeline examples.

Consistency semantics specification for versioned spatial database: snapshot isolation guarantees, conflict resolution algorithm for concurrent updates, eventual consistency model with version causality.

Performance acceptance criteria and latency bounds: maximum latency for incremental feature extraction, query response times, change notification delivery latency. Profiling results demonstrating compliance.

Scope definition for 'agreed set of site features': list of geometry primitives (points, lines, polygons, meshes), safety attributes (hazard zones, restricted areas, speed limits), operational constraints (access rules, equipment restrictions). Schema extension mechanism for future features.

Change notification subscription model specification: pub-sub semantics, filtering capabilities (by feature type, region, change type), delivery guarantees (at-least-once, exactly-once), subscription lifecycle management.

Sensor validation boundary specification: which validation checks (outlier detection, calibration, quality flags) occur in Vehicle/Teleoperation vs. NGHE Solution. Acceptance criteria for sensor data quality.

Interface contract specifications for Telemetry (raw format, normalization rules), SafetyAlert (constraint violation schema, delivery semantics), Tasking (snapshot format, versioning metadata, change notification format), Network Link (query protocol, consistency guarantees).
Test plans and acceptance criteria for schema versioning, consistency semantics, performance targets, and change notification reliability.
